text:
There once was a girl named Willow who lived deep in a forest where talking animals were commonplace. One sunny morning, Willow discovered an ancient tree near her home filled with tiny bird songs.

She befriended Owl the wise owl, and together they listened to the countless species nesting within the tree. They learned things like how flamingos floated their egos on water birds after dipping them into pancakes, and how hummingbirds fed their babies tiny insects while humming.

One day, Willow decided to host an owl fest. She baked owl cookies, knitted owl hats, and decorated with twig wreaths made by hedgebirds. The creatures seemed thrilled.

Soon, a big gray owl swooped in with a sleepy buzz. Willow welcomed him with open wings. They stayed up late listening to nighttime songs from bats.

The owl learned about Willow’s love of birds, and together they watched a flock of hummingbirds race through cherry blossoms. Willow felt grateful.

As they drifted off to sleep under the moonlit sky
